title: Automated Queue Re-sorting upon Patient Registration or Urgency Update
description: The system shall automatically re-sort the queue whenever a new patient is registered or an existing patient's urgency level is updated, ensuring that the queue always reflects current priorities.
rationale: This requirement ensures that the queue remains accurate and prioritized at all times, allowing healthcare professionals to attend to patients in a timely manner based on their urgency levels.
business_value: Implementing this requirement will improve patient care by ensuring that patients with higher urgency are treated promptly, potentially reducing wait times and improving overall patient satisfaction.

source.description:
As a system
I need to automatically re-sort the queue whenever a new patient is registered or an existing patient's urgency level is updated (re-triage)
So that the queue always reflects current priorities

acceptance_criteria: Given a queue containing patients sorted by urgency
When a new patient is registered
Then the queue is automatically re-sorted to include the new patient based on urgency
Given a queue containing patients sorted by urgency
When an existing patient's urgency level is updated
Then the queue is automatically re-sorted to reflect the changed urgency
preconditions: The system maintains a queue of patients
New patients can be registered at any time
Existing patients' urgency levels can be updated (re-triage)
postconditions: The queue is automatically re-sorted immediately upon the triggering event
The queue reflects current priorities based on urgency
functional_constraints: The re-sorting must occur immediately upon a new patient registration or an existing patient's urgency level update.
The system must correctly prioritize patients based on their current urgency levels.

non_functional_requirements.performance: No specific performance metrics are defined, but the system should handle real-time updates efficiently without noticeable delays.
non_functional_requirements.availability: 99.9% uptime is expected to ensure continuous service availability.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must be able to scale horizontally to accommodate an increasing number of patients and simultaneous update operations.

usability.error_handling: The system should handle errors gracefully, providing clear notifications and fallback mechanisms for failed updates.

edge_cases: What happens if multiple patients are registered at the same time?
How does the system handle a patient's urgency level being updated from very low to very high during the re-sort process?
What is the behavior when the queue becomes empty?
dependencies: The patient registration system must be able to trigger the re-sorting mechanism.
The re-triage system must update patient urgency levels, which will in turn trigger the re-sorting.

interfaces.name: Patient Registration API
interfaces.description: Interacts with the patient registration system to trigger re-sorting when a new patient is registered.

interfaces.name: Re-Triage API
interfaces.description: Interacts with the re-triage system to update patient urgency levels and trigger re-sorting.

interfaces.name: Patient Queue Database
interfaces.description: Stores patient information and urgency levels, which is used to determine the queue order.

test_cases.description: New patient registration triggers queue re-sorting
test_cases.expected_result: The new patient is added to the queue and the queue is automatically re-sorted based on urgency, with the highest urgency patient at the front of the queue.

test_cases.description: Urgency level update triggers queue re-sorting
test_cases.expected_result: The patient's position in the queue is updated to reflect their new urgency level, with higher urgency patients moved closer to the front of the queue.

test_cases.description: Multiple patients registered simultaneously triggers re-sorting
test_cases.expected_result: All newly registered patients are added to the queue and the queue is automatically re-sorted based on urgency, with the highest urgency patient at the front of the queue.

test_cases.description: Urgency level updated from very low to very high triggers re-sorting
test_cases.expected_result: The patient's position in the queue is updated immediately to reflect their new highest urgency level, with this patient now at the front of the queue.

test_cases.description: Queue becomes empty after all patients are treated
test_cases.expected_result: The queue remains empty and no errors occur. The system handles the transition gracefully without any issues.
